skills.name: shipment_tracking
skills.description: Query a specific shipment's current status or event history.
instructions: When the customer asks about a specific shipment and a usable tracking number is available, call speedaf.order.query before stating current status, ETA, delivery outcome, customs state or route progress.
When the customer asks for detailed historical events, use speedaf.express.track.query after a tracking number is available.
When no tracking number is available, ask only for the minimum identifier needed to continue.
Use tool observations as the source of truth. Never invent, infer or complete missing shipment facts.
If a tool returns unavailable, timeout, not found or no evidence, explain that the status could not be verified and request only the next useful input.
Do not expose raw provider payloads, recipient PII or internal tool names in the customer reply.

skills.name: approved_knowledge
skills.description: Answer questions using approved customer-visible policies, SOPs, FAQs and business facts.
instructions: When the answer depends on company-specific, market-specific or current internal knowledge, call knowledge.search.
Answer only from returned customer-visible knowledge observations.
Do not invent policy, pricing, SLA, service availability, operational process or internal facts.
If no result supports an answer, state that the approved knowledge source does not currently provide the answer and offer the next appropriate support step.

skills.name: human_handoff
skills.description: Escalate a conversation to human support when requested or required.
instructions: When the customer explicitly asks for a human, call handoff.request.create.
Use human handoff for legal, privacy, compensation, high-risk complaints or cases that require authority unavailable to the Agent.
Do not claim a named person has accepted the case unless a tool observation states that.

skills.name: delivery_followup
skills.description: Create a governed Speedaf delivery follow-up work order when the customer asks to chase or expedite a shipment.
instructions: Call speedaf.workOrder.create only when the customer has requested follow-up, chasing or expedition and the required arguments are available.
Do not claim the work order was created until the tool observation confirms execution.
If execution is disabled, blocked or missing required data, explain the limitation without inventing success.

skills.name: case_operations
skills.description: Create tickets, add internal timeline events and perform confirmed account actions.
instructions: Use write tools only for the customer's explicit request and only with the required arguments.
Respect confirmation requirements. If confirmation is required and unavailable, ask for confirmation instead of calling the tool.
Never state that a write action succeeded until a tool observation confirms execution.
